sequenceOverview.escalationRule: Stillness and observation in Scene 1, invasive forward drift in Scene 2, uninterrupted propulsion in Scene 3, weaponized inevitability in Scene 4.

scenes.title: House and Woman
scenes.purpose: Establish the house as a real, specific home and the Woman as its quiet center.
scenes.locationAndTime: Exterior field and interior room of the same rural house, overcast day.
participants: The Woman only; the Soldier appears only as a portrait.
scenes.whatChanges: The house shifts from distant object to intimate interior memory-space. The portrait is introduced as the next threshold.
scenes.transitionOut: The portrait fills the frame and stops behaving like a wall object.

shots.cameraMotion: Very slow forward drift across the field toward the house.
shots.subjectAction: The house sits still in an empty field; no visible human movement outside.
continuityDependencies: Whitewashed walls, dark pitched roof, single chimney, two front-facing windows, overcast daylight.
shots.intendedTransition: Opening anchor image; the geometry of the facade must be memorable for the final FPV return.
shots.firstFrameState: The house is small in frame, surrounded by muted field grass and soft gray sky.
shots.lastFrameState: The house is larger and centered, with the front facade clearly legible.

shots.framing: Medium-close interior profile or three-quarter view of the Woman seated and knitting.
shots.cameraMotion: Subtle locked-off frame or almost imperceptible inward creep.
shots.subjectAction: The Woman knits slowly; only her hands, shoulders, and breath move.
continuityDependencies: Same overcast daylight motivated through window light.
Same wardrobe throughout.
Portrait visible behind or near her.
Wool yarn and wooden interior details present.
shots.intendedTransition: Cut from exterior to interior by matching stillness; this shot establishes the domestic rhythm that the portrait shot will violate.
shots.firstFrameState: Her weathered hands and knitting occupy the visual foreground, room details soft behind.
shots.lastFrameState: The portrait becomes more compositionally dominant behind her as the viewer's attention shifts upward.

shots.framing: Portrait-centered push-in, beginning as a medium shot of wall area and ending near full-frame portrait.
shots.cameraMotion: Deliberate slow push toward the framed portrait.
shots.subjectAction: The Woman remains seated and nearly still at the edge of frame or just out of frame; the portrait does not yet move.
continuityDependencies: Portrait frame must look aged and modest.
The Soldier's face must match later battlefield identity.
Room texture and window light remain natural and subdued.
shots.intendedTransition: The push becomes the bridge into Scene 2; the final frame should be composed to let the portrait image become the battlefield without a jarring reset.
shots.firstFrameState: Portrait hangs in its room context, clearly a physical object on a plaster wall.
shots.lastFrameState: The portrait image nearly fills the screen, with frame edges beginning to disappear into darkness and grain.

scenes.title: Portrait to Battlefield
scenes.purpose: Turn memory into lived war and make the Soldier emotionally immediate.
scenes.locationAndTime: The image-world of the portrait opening into an older battlefield, daytime through smoke.
scenes.whatChanges: A static monochrome image becomes moving monochrome memory, then crosses into color and danger.
scenes.transitionIn: Continue the push from the portrait until the image gains depth and breath.
scenes.transitionOut: The Soldier's forward path resolves into the tank surface and then an opening into machinery.

shots.framing: Tight portrait-to-tableau transformation, starting close on the Soldier's face and widening slightly as depth appears behind him.
shots.cameraMotion: Continuous forward creep into the portrait plane.
shots.subjectAction: The portrait subtly comes alive; smoke or drifting particulate begins to move while the Soldier holds the same pose for a moment before animation starts.
continuityDependencies: Exact facial anchors from the portrait.
Image begins in black and white.
No abrupt change of costume or age.
shots.intendedTransition: Should feel like the same shot as S1-SH03 completed; last moment cues the Soldier's first real motion into S2-SH05.
shots.firstFrameState: Almost flat monochrome portrait texture, as if seen on paper or canvas.
shots.lastFrameState: The Soldier is now inside a dimensional monochrome battlefield with smoke moving around him.

shots.framing: Medium-wide to medium tracking view on the Soldier running forward through battlefield chaos.
shots.cameraMotion: Urgent forward-tracking pursuit that stays on the Soldier's path.
shots.subjectAction: The Soldier runs through smoke, mud, and blast pressure; during the shot the monochrome image gradually blooms into muted color.
continuityDependencies: Older-war uniform only.
Same face as portrait.
Battlefield remains daytime through smoke.
Forward motion must line up with the tank entrance in the next shot.
shots.intendedTransition: The tank must appear on the Soldier's forward line so the cut or seamless passage into Scene 3 feels spatially inevitable rather than symbolic only.
shots.firstFrameState: The Soldier breaks into motion in near-monochrome, smoke swallowing the background.
shots.lastFrameState: Muted color is fully present and the tank body dominates the forward path as camera closes on it.

scenes.title: Tank to FPV
scenes.purpose: Bridge older mechanized war into contemporary remote violence through uninterrupted forward force.
scenes.locationAndTime: Exterior battlefield surface into tank interior space, then out through the barrel into open air above the same rural landscape.
participants: No stable human subject; the camera inherits the war machine's trajectory.
scenes.whatChanges: The viewer stops following a person and becomes the movement of a weapon system.
scenes.transitionIn: Enter the tank on the Soldier's forward vector.
scenes.transitionOut: The barrel exit becomes an FPV flight line aimed at the house.

shots.framing: Extreme forward rush into tank armor and interior tunnel geometry.
shots.cameraMotion: Aggressive continuous plunge through metal, seams, darkness, and mechanical texture.
shots.subjectAction: No human action foregrounded; the shot is about spatial conversion from battlefield exterior into machine interior.
continuityDependencies: The entry vector must match the end of S2-SH05.
Tank design should read as older-war hardware, not futuristic.
Motion stays smooth rather than montage-like.
shots.intendedTransition: Either hidden cut on darkness or metal, or a seamless morph. This is the sequence hinge that converts observer perspective into projectile perspective.
shots.firstFrameState: Tank hull fills the frame at close range, color and smoke still visible.
shots.lastFrameState: The image resolves into a centered barrel-like tunnel with a bright circular exit ahead.

shots.framing: First-person barrel exit into low-altitude FPV flight over the field.
shots.cameraMotion: Explosive release from tunnel into open-air acceleration.
shots.subjectAction: The camera bursts from the barrel and stabilizes into contemporary FPV-like forward flight with no interface graphics.
continuityDependencies: Daylight must match the opening exterior.
Field orientation should allow the same house to appear in the next shot.
No HUD or digital overlays.
shots.intendedTransition: This is the modern-war reveal; the last frame must already orient the viewer toward the house corridor used in Scene 4.
shots.firstFrameState: Bright circular exit at the end of the barrel fills the screen.
shots.lastFrameState: The field horizon locks in and the house direction becomes spatially readable ahead.

scenes.title: Strike
scenes.purpose: Return to the opening house and destroy it only after recognition is secure.
scenes.locationAndTime: The same rural field and house from Scene 1, same overcast day.
participants: The house as target; the Woman is implied inside, not shown.
scenes.whatChanges: The remembered home becomes the endpoint of a weapon trajectory.
scenes.transitionIn: Maintain uninterrupted FPV motion from the barrel exit.
scenes.transitionOut: End on obliteration and debris bloom, not aftermath coverage.

shots.framing: Low fast FPV approach on the house, slightly rising as needed to keep facade legible.
shots.cameraMotion: Relentless forward rush with slight tactical corrections, no ornamental flourishes.
shots.subjectAction: The house rapidly grows in frame; grass, fence, or ground texture streaks by underneath.
continuityDependencies: House architecture must exactly match S1-SH01.
Daylight and field palette remain consistent.
The shot must hold recognition for at least a beat before impact.
shots.intendedTransition: The purpose is confirmation; the end of the shot should place the viewer milliseconds before collision so the final shot can either continue seamlessly or cut on imminent contact.
shots.firstFrameState: The house is visible ahead at distance, clearly the same building from the opening.
shots.lastFrameState: The facade nearly fills frame, windows and wall texture identifiable just before impact.

shots.framing: Impact close with immediate engulfing detonation.
shots.cameraMotion: Uninterrupted collision path ending in violent bloom and image breakup.
shots.subjectAction: Collision triggers an orange-white explosion, debris, dust, and collapsing fragments; readability lasts a fraction before obliteration.
continuityDependencies: Impact must occur on the same facade established in S4-SH08.
Explosion should not stylize into blockbuster fireball.
The house must still be recognizable at the first instant of contact.
shots.intendedTransition: Terminal end shot; no need for a separate aftermath unless later revision demands it.
shots.firstFrameState: The front wall and window area are still recognizable at the point of imminent contact.
shots.lastFrameState: The frame is consumed by blast light, dust, and debris with orientation collapsing into white-orange chaos.
